name: Southwestern Egg Rolls
instructions:
1. Rub half of the Vegetable Oil over the Chicken Breast. Cook chicken in a medium saucepan over medium heat until meat is no longer pink and juices run clear, about 5 minutes per side. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 165 degrees F (74 degrees C). The chicken should feel firm to the touch and release clear juices when pierced. Remove from heat and set aside to rest.

2. Heat the remaining Vegetable Oil in the same saucepan over medium heat. Add the green Onion and Bell Pepper. Cook and stir until softened and fragrant, about 5 minutes. The vegetables should be tender but still have a slight bite.

3. Dice the cooked chicken into small cubes and add to the pan with the vegetables. Stir in the Corn, Black Beans, Spinach, Jalapeño peppers, Fresh Parsley, Cumin, Chili Powder, Salt, and Cayenne Pepper. Cook and stir until everything is well combined and heated through, about 5 minutes. The spinach should be wilted and the mixture aromatic. Remove from heat and stir in the Monterey Jack cheese, allowing it to melt into the warm filling.

4. Wrap the Tortillas in a clean, lightly damp cloth. Microwave on high until hot and pliable, about 1 minute. The tortillas should be soft and easy to fold without cracking.

5. Spoon equal amounts of the filling onto each tortilla, placing it in the center. Fold in the ends of the tortillas first, then roll tightly around the mixture to create a sealed package. Secure each roll with toothpicks to prevent them from opening during freezing and frying.

6. Arrange the rolls in a single layer in a dish, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and place in the freezer. Freeze for at least 4 hours or until completely solid. Freezing helps the rolls hold their shape during frying.

7. Heat the deep-frying Vegetable Oil in a large, deep skillet to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Carefully add the frozen egg rolls and deep-fry until dark golden brown and crispy, about 10 minutes each. The filling should be heated through and the exterior crispy. Drain on paper towels to remove excess oil before serving hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook the Chicken Breast with half the Vegetable Oil until fully cooked and juices run clear.

2. Sauté the green Onion and Bell Pepper in the remaining oil until tender.

3. Mix in the diced chicken, Corn, Black Beans, Spinach, Jalapeño, Fresh Parsley, and all spices. Stir in the Monterey Jack cheese until melted.

4. Warm the Tortillas and fill each with the mixture. Roll tightly and secure with toothpicks.

5. Freeze the rolls for at least 4 hours.

6. Deep-fry the frozen rolls in hot oil until golden brown and crispy.
